an1.11:0.2: 2. The Chapter on Abandoning the Hindrances
an1.11:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing on account of which unarisen sensual desire arises and arisen sensual desire increases and expands so much as a beautiful *nimitta*.
an1.11:1.2: For one who attends inappropriately [*ayoniso manasi karoto*] to a beautiful *nimitta*, bhikkhus, unarisen sensual desire arises and arisen sensual desire increases and expands.”
an1.12:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing on account of which unarisen ill will arises and arisen ill will increases and expands so much as a *nimitta* of resistance.
an1.12:1.2: For one who attends inappropriately [*ayoniso manasi karoto*] to a *nimitta* of resistance, bhikkhus, unarisen ill will arises and arisen ill will increases and expands.”
an1.13:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing on account of which unarisen sloth and torpor arise and arisen sloth and torpor increase and expand so much as discontent, lethargy, lazy stretching, drowsiness after meals, and mental sluggishness.
an1.13:1.2: For one with a sluggish mind [*citta*], bhikkhus, unarisen sloth and torpor arise and arisen sloth and torpor increase and expand.”
an1.14:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing on account of which unarisen restlessness and remorse arise and arisen restlessness and remorse increase and expand so much as an unsettled mind [*citta*].
an1.14:1.2: For one with an unsettled mind [*citta*], bhikkhus, unarisen restlessness and remorse arise and arisen restlessness and remorse increase and expand.”
an1.15:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing on account of which unarisen doubt arises and arisen doubt increases and expands so much as inappropriate attention [*ayonisomanasikāro*].
an1.15:1.2: For one who attends inappropriately [*ayoniso manasi karoto*], bhikkhus, unarisen doubt arises and arisen doubt increases and expands.”
an1.16:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing on account of which unarisen sensual desire does not arise and arisen sensual desire is abandoned so much as an unattractive *nimitta*.
an1.16:1.2: For one who attends appropriately [*yoniso manasi karoto*] to an unattractive *nimitta*, bhikkhus, unarisen sensual desire does not arise and arisen sensual desire is abandoned.”
an1.17:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing on account of which unarisen ill will does not arise and arisen ill will is abandoned so much as the liberation of the mind by goodwill [*mettā*].
an1.17:1.2: For one who attends appropriately [*yoniso manasi karoto*] to the liberation of the mind by goodwill [*mettā*], bhikkhus, unarisen ill will does not arise and arisen ill will is abandoned.”
an1.18:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing on account of which unarisen sloth and torpor do not arise and arisen sloth and torpor are abandoned so much as the element of initiative, the element of launching, and the element of exertion.
an1.18:1.2: For one with aroused energy, bhikkhus, unarisen sloth and torpor do not arise and arisen sloth and torpor are abandoned.”
an1.19:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing on account of which unarisen restlessness and remorse do not arise and arisen restlessness and remorse are abandoned so much as the settling of the mind [*citta*].
an1.19:1.2: For one with a settled mind [*citta*], bhikkhus, unarisen restlessness and remorse do not arise and arisen restlessness and remorse are abandoned.”
an1.20:1.1: “I do not see, bhikkhus, any other single thing on account of which unarisen doubt does not arise and arisen doubt is abandoned so much as appropriate attention [*yonisomanasikāro*].
an1.20:1.2: For one who attends appropriately [*yoniso manasi karoto*], bhikkhus, unarisen doubt does not arise and arisen doubt is abandoned.”
an1.20:1.4: The Chapter on Abandoning the Hindrances is the second.
